rag_rules: Generate output guarantees for visible ports using concrete bounds or named relations from the requirement.
Place all guarantees in component type annexes when referencing visible features, not implementation-level bindings.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Express range constraints using logical operators (>=, <=, and, or) and avoid implicit AADL property references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Wrap AGREE content in annex agree {** ... **};
Declare constants with const name : type = value; and equations with eq name : type = expression;
Use bool-typed helpers for range checks, e.g., eq in_range : bool = x >= min and x <= max;
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent paths or implementation-only names in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
